name: Orunmila
description: Orunmila, also known as Ifa, is a prominent Orisha in Yoruba religion and its diaspora. He is revered as the Orisha of wisdom, knowledge, and divination. Orunmila witnessed the creation of the universe and possesses intimate knowledge of destiny, both individual and collective. He is the only Orisha who was present at the beginning of time and knows the fate of all things. Orunmila's primary function is to guide individuals towards their destiny through divination, using the sacred Opele chain or Ikin (palm nuts). He reveals the past, present, and future, offering solutions to problems and guiding followers to make informed decisions. Orunmila is not a warrior Orisha; his power lies in his profound understanding of the universe and his ability to interpret the divine will. He is a benevolent deity, dedicated to helping humanity achieve spiritual growth and fulfillment. Orunmila is considered the second in command to Olodumare, the supreme being, and acts as his intermediary in matters of divination and destiny. His teachings emphasize ethical conduct, respect for tradition, and the importance of seeking wisdom in navigating life's challenges. He is the patron of diviners (Babalawo) and divineresses (Iyanifa), who are trained in the complex system of Ifa divination. Orunmila's influence extends far beyond divination; he is a symbol of intellectual and spiritual enlightenment.
subtitle: Orisha of Wisdom and Divination

keyMyths.title: Orunmila Witnesses Creation
keyMyths.description: Orunmila was present at the creation of the universe, giving him unique insight into the workings of fate. He saw how Olodumare structured the cosmos and the destiny of all beings.
keyMyths.source: Oral traditions and Ifa literary corpus

keyMyths.title: Orunmila Teaches Ifa
keyMyths.description: Orunmila revealed the secrets of Ifa divination to humanity, allowing them to understand their destiny and navigate life's challenges. This knowledge is passed down through generations of diviners.
keyMyths.source: Oral traditions and Ifa literary corpus

keyMyths.title: Orunmila and the Lost Ase
keyMyths.description: A myth tells of Orunmila retrieving lost Ase (divine power) for humanity, emphasizing his role as a benefactor and protector of spiritual well-being. This showed his importance to humanity.
keyMyths.source: Oral traditions and Ifa literary corpus

worshipPractices.name: Divination
worshipPractices.description: Ifa divination, using the Opele chain or Ikin nuts, is central to Orunmila's worship. Babalawos (Ifa priests) interpret the patterns to reveal destinies and offer guidance.

worshipPractices.name: Offerings
worshipPractices.description: Offerings of kola nuts, palm oil, animals (depending on tradition), and other symbolic items are made to Orunmila to seek his blessings and favor.

festivals.name: Ifa Festival
festivals.description: Celebrated by Ifa devotees worldwide, this festival honors Orunmila through rituals, prayers, and feasting, reaffirming their connection to wisdom and destiny.

cultural.modernLegacy: Orunmila's influence is visible in contemporary Yoruba religious practices, Ifa divination's continued use, and its impact on Afro-Caribbean religions like Santería and Candomblé. His wisdom is sought by many.

extendedContent.title: Origins & Birth
extendedContent.content: Orunmila's origins are deeply rooted in Yoruba cosmology. He is often described as having been present at the creation of the universe, witnessing Olodumare (the Supreme God) establish order. While not 'born' in the conventional sense, Orunmila's existence is considered primordial and intrinsic to the universe's fabric. He is sometimes depicted as a son of Olodumare, emphasizing his divine status and unique access to divine knowledge. His emergence signifies the beginning of wisdom and the possibility of understanding destiny. Orunmila's role was to be the divine witness and record keeper, making him the ultimate authority on fate and the intricacies of existence. This positions him as the foundation for Ifa divination and the understanding of the Yoruba worldview.

extendedContent.title: Major Myths & Stories
extendedContent.content: Many myths surround Orunmila, illustrating his wisdom and power. One prominent story recounts how he outsmarted Esu (the trickster Orisha) on several occasions, showcasing his superior intellect and understanding of cosmic forces. Another tale describes his journey to various communities, spreading the knowledge of Ifa divination and establishing the role of the Babalawo. Orunmila's interactions with other Orishas are also central to Yoruba mythology. He often acts as a mediator and advisor, resolving conflicts and guiding them toward harmonious solutions. These narratives emphasize his role as a wise counselor and a force for balance within the pantheon. The stories also highlight the importance of seeking Orunmila's guidance to navigate life's challenges and understand one's destiny.

extendedContent.title: Worship & Cult
extendedContent.content: Worship of Orunmila centers around Ifa divination, a complex system of knowledge and practice. Babalawos, initiated priests of Ifa, are the primary intermediaries between Orunmila and devotees. They use Opele chains or Ikin nuts to interpret patterns and reveal messages from Orunmila, providing guidance on various aspects of life, from health and relationships to career and spiritual growth. Offerings are also made to Orunmila, including kola nuts, palm oil, and animals (depending on tradition and circumstance). These offerings are intended to show reverence and gratitude, as well as to seek blessings and protection. The Ifa literary corpus, a vast collection of verses and stories, forms the foundation of Orunmila's worship, providing insights into Yoruba cosmology, ethics, and the nature of destiny.

extendedContent.title: Legacy & Influence
extendedContent.content: Orunmila's legacy extends far beyond the Yoruba homeland. His influence is deeply embedded in Afro-Caribbean religions such as Santería in Cuba and Candomblé in Brazil, where he is known as Orula. In these traditions, Ifa divination remains a central practice, and Orunmila is revered as a source of wisdom and guidance. The ethical principles associated with Orunmila, such as truthfulness, integrity, and respect for elders, continue to shape the moral compass of many communities. The study of Ifa is growing, with scholars and practitioners worldwide seeking to understand its complex system of knowledge. Orunmila's enduring presence in art, literature, and music reflects his profound impact on culture and spirituality. His wisdom continues to inspire and guide individuals seeking to understand their place in the world and navigate the complexities of life.

shortDescription: Orunmila, also known as Ifa, is the Yoruba Orisha of wisdom, knowledge, and divination. He knows the destiny of all things and guides people to fulfilling their fate.
